title: lavandula canariensis subsp. canariae
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. canariae, also known as the Big Island Kanahia or Kanahia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or willow family. This plant is native to the Canary Islands and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lavandula canariensis subsp. canariae include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce anxiety and stress, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
